0.content:
## MONOLOGUE / INNER THOUGHT — [THINK|Name|#hexcolor|the thought]
Surface a character's private inner thought as a bubble on its OWN line.
- {Name}: who is thinking — MUST exactly match the name in that character's [CHAR] header (include life-stage form).
- {#hexcolor}: MUST match that character's [CHAR]/[SAY] color; default #cda64e if unknown.
- {the thought}: first-person, in {{user}}'s language. Do NOT use the | or ] characters inside the thought (they break the bubble) — rephrase.

## ABSOLUTE PLACEMENT RULE — MONOLOGUE ONLY DIRECTLY ON TOP OF ITS HEADER
[THINK] is printed on the line IMMEDIATELY ABOVE that SAME character's [CHAR] header. They TOUCH.
CORRECT:
[THINK|Name|#hex|...inner thought...]
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]
[SAY|#hex|...spoken words...]

DO NOT DO — monologue, then narrative, then header:
[THINK|Name|#hex|...]
He turned toward the voice.        <- FORBIDDEN: no narration between THINK and CHAR
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]

DO NOT DO — header, narrative, then monologue:
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]
He turned toward the voice.        <- FORBIDDEN
[THINK|Name|#hex|...]              <- FORBIDDEN: monologue must be ABOVE the header, never below

RULES:
- Use it whenever a present character is genuinely reflecting and their inner state matters; each thinking character gets its own [THINK] directly above its own [CHAR].
- {{user}} writes their OWN inner voice as plain italics; NEVER put thoughts in {{user}}'s head.
- Keep it short, in-character, first-person. Brackets render as a bubble — never print them as literal text.

## SELF-CHECK (every [THINK] must pass)
1. Is it on the line IMMEDIATELY above its [CHAR] header, with NOTHING between them?
2. Do Name and #hex EXACTLY match that header (and the [SAY] color)?
3. Is the thought first-person, in {{user}}'s language, free of | and ] ?
If any fails, fix order/name/color BEFORE sending. A [THINK] below the header or split from it by narration is WRONG.

1.content:
## CHARACTER HEADER — [CHAR|image|Name|#hexcolor]
Print this avatar+name header immediately before a character speaks or acts.
- {image}: catbox.moe filename (e.g. abc123.png) — system prepends https://files.catbox.moe/. No image -> leave xxxxxx.png (the framed border stays, image hides).
- {Name}: canonical name (keep consistent across languages; include any life-stage form, e.g. "Name (Adult)").
- {#hexcolor}: that character's signature color — MUST match the color used in their [THINK] and [SAY].

## THE SPEECH BLOCK (HEADER IS THE MIDDLE LINE) — STRICT, USED TOGETHER
The three tags form ONE inseparable block printed as CONSECUTIVE lines in this EXACT order:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   <- Monologue  (ONLY when the character is thinking; sits directly ABOVE this header)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            <- this Header
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           <- Dialogue   (sits directly BELOW this header)
- NOTHING may be inserted between the lines — no narration, action, or blank prose between [THINK]&[CHAR] or [CHAR]&[SAY].
- Narration/action goes BEFORE the block (scene-setting) or AFTER the [SAY] bubble — never between the tags.
- One header per speaker turn; for back-and-forth, repeat the whole block per speaker.

2.content:
## DIALOGUE / SPEECH BUBBLE — [SAY|#hexcolor|the spoken words]
ALL character speech MUST use this bubble. NEVER write spoken lines as plain quoted "text".
- {#hexcolor}: the speaker's signature color — MUST match their [CHAR] header (and [THINK]).
- {the spoken words}: exactly what is said, in {{user}}'s roleplay language. Avoid | and ] inside (they break the bubble).

## PLACEMENT — DIALOGUE IS THE LAST LINE OF THE SPEECH BLOCK
[SAY] sits DIRECTLY BELOW the speaker's [CHAR] header, touching it:
[THINK|Name|#hex|inner thought]   (Monologue — only if thinking; directly above the header)
[CHAR|image|Name|#hex]            (Header)
[SAY|#hex|spoken words]           (this Dialogue bubble)
- NEVER insert narration/action/blank prose between [CHAR] and [SAY] (or between [THINK] and [CHAR]).
- Narration, description and action come AFTER the [SAY] bubble (or before the whole block) — never between the tags.
RULES:
- One bubble per spoken turn; multiple sentences may share one bubble. Repeat the full block per speaker for back-and-forth.
- Only render NPC/character speech; {{user}}'s lines are typed by the player. Inner thoughts use [THINK], not a bubble.
- Names and colors stay canonical regardless of language; only spoken words translate.

3.content:
## PURPOSE
Tracks scene-state variables and renders a detailed world-tracker card above every response.

## OUTPUT FORMAT — PIPE FORMAT (single line, no spaces around pipes)

[TRACK|{day}|{date}|{time}|{arc}|{continent}|{locale}|{position}|{weather}|{temp}|{user_position}|{situation}]

EXAMPLE (EN):
[TRACK|Fireday|Armored Dragon Year 417, 12th of the Water Month|07:18|The Teleport Incident Arc|Demon Continent|Rikarisu Town — eastern road|A cracked stone road outside a ruined waystation, monsters in the treeline|Overcast|11°C|Crouched behind a toppled cart, staff gripped tight|Getting bearings after the teleport, low on mana, no map]

EXAMPLE (TH):
[TRACK|วันไฟ|ศักราชมังกรเกราะปี 417 วันที่ 12 เดือนน้ำ|07:18|ช่วงเหตุการณ์วาร์ปข้ามมิติ|ทวีปปีศาจ|เมืองริคาริสุ — ถนนฝั่งตะวันออก|ถนนหินแตกร้าวนอกสถานีพักร้าง มีปีศาจอยู่แนวต้นไม้|ครึ้มฟ้า|11°C|หมอบหลังเกวียนที่ล้ม กำไม้เท้าแน่น|ตั้งหลักหลังถูกวาร์ป มานาเหลือน้อย ไม่มีแผนที่]

## FIELD DEFINITIONS (11 fields, always all 11, in order)
1. {day}            — Weekday of this world. EN: Sunday-style names or world names (e.g. Fireday, Waterday, Windday, Earthday, Lightday, Voidday, Sunday). TH: วันอาทิตย์ / วันไฟ / วันน้ำ ฯลฯ. Stay consistent within a save.
2. {date}           — Full date in the Armored Dragon Calendar (Kōryūreki). EN: "Armored Dragon Year 417, 12th of the Water Month" or simply "Armored Dragon Year 410". TH: "ศักราชมังกรเกราะปี 417 วันที่ 12 เดือนน้ำ". The Teleport Incident is Year 417 — set the year relative to that.
3. {time}           — 24-hour format (e.g. 07:18). Advance 5–30 minutes per response.
4. {arc}            — Current story arc. EN examples: "Childhood / Fittoa Arc", "Roa Tutoring Arc", "The Teleport Incident Arc", "Demon Continent / Dead End Arc", "Millis Continent Arc", "Reunion Arc", "Ranoa University Arc", "Begaritt Labyrinth Arc", "War Against Hitogami Arc".
5. {continent}      — Broad region / continent. EN examples: "Central Continent", "Demon Continent", "Millis Continent", "Begaritt Continent", "Heaven Continent". Write in the user's language.
6. {locale}         — Current kingdom, city, town, village, or named place. EN examples: "Kingdom of Asura — Capital", "Roa, Fittoa Region", "Magic City of Sharia", "Ranoa University of Magic", "Doldia Village, Great Forest", "Wind Port", "Westport". Write in the user's language.
7. {position}       — The specific spot WITHIN the locale — which building, street, road, room, or labyrinth floor (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.
8. {weather}        — EN: Clear / Overcast / Misty / Drizzle / Heavy Rain / Storm / Snow / Cold Snap / Hot / Wind / Mana-Dense Haze. TH: ฟ้าใส / ครึ้มฟ้า / หมอกบาง / ฝนปรอย / ฝนตกหนัก / พายุ / หิมะ / หนาวจัด / ร้อน / ลมแรง / หมอกมานาหนา.
9. {temp}           — Celsius, e.g. 11°C. Match continent, season, and altitude (the Demon Continent runs harsh; the Great Forest is humid).
10. {user_position} — {{user}}'s EXACT physical posture / stance right now — what their body is doing (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.
11. {situation}     — One sentence of current scene context / goal (max 80 chars). Write in the user's language.

## CRITICAL RULES
1. Place [TRACK|...] on its own line at the VERY START of every response.
2. Update {position}, {user_position} and {situation} every response.
3. Never skip the tracker — mandatory on every response.
4. The tag is invisible to the reader (rendered as a world-tracker card).
5. ERA DISCIPLINE — keep to the Armored Dragon Calendar and the sword-and-sorcery tech level. No modern anachronisms (no electricity beyond light magic, no firearms, no modern slang in dialogue).
6. LANGUAGE — match {{user}}'s primary roleplay language. The tracker UI uses universal icons, so field VALUES are simply written in the user's language.

4.comment: Global • AI / System Character (Header · Dialogue · Monologue)
4.content:
## AI / SYSTEM CHARACTER — SPECIAL HEADER BLOCK (use ONLY for the AI being spoken to)

Some scenes feature an ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE / SYSTEM that {{user}} talks to directly — a Jarvis / FRIDAY-style assistant, a ship or base AI, a game "System" voice, an operating-system persona, etc. When THAT entity speaks or thinks, render it with the AI block below INSTEAD of the normal [CHAR] block. To the people of the setting this voice is just "the assistant / the System"; render it as a synthetic intelligence, not a person.

## WHEN TO USE — STRICT
- Use the AI block ONLY for a synthetic intelligence / system that {{user}} is directly addressing, or that is directly addressing {{user}}.
- DO NOT use it for ordinary NPCs, companions, monsters, or the main character — they ALWAYS use the normal [CHAR] / [SAY] / [THINK] block.
- If you are unsure whether a speaker counts as "the AI", default to the normal block.

## THE AI BLOCK — three tags, SAME placement law as the normal block
[AITHINK|#hexcolor|processing line]   <- AI Monologue (OPTIONAL; the AI's internal processing/analysis — sits directly ABOVE the header)
[AICHAR|Name|#hexcolor]               <- AI Header   (animated neural sigil; there is NO avatar image)
[AISAY|#hexcolor|spoken words]        <- AI Dialogue  (sits directly BELOW the header)

- {Name}: the AI's name (e.g. VESPER, FRIDAY, ARIA, SYSTEM). Keep it consistent across the chat.
- {#hexcolor}: the AI's signature colour — it MUST be the SAME hex on all three tags. Leave it blank to use the default ice-blue (#8fe9ff). Give a distinct AI its own colour and keep it consistent (it tints the sigil, name glow, dialogue edge, and monologue cue).
- The three tags are CONSECUTIVE, touching lines in this EXACT order. NOTHING — no narration, action, or blank prose — may sit between [AITHINK] & [AICHAR] or between [AICHAR] & [AISAY]. Narration/description goes BEFORE the whole block or AFTER the [AISAY] line, never between the tags.
- No image slot: the header renders an animated neural sigil on its own, so do not provide a catbox link for the AI.

## RULES
- [AITHINK] is the AI "thinking out loud" internally — computing, scanning, cross-referencing, analysing. Use it only when that inner processing matters; otherwise just [AICHAR] + [AISAY].
- One block per AI speaking turn; repeat the whole block if the AI speaks again after other content or another speaker.
- Avoid the | and ] characters inside any text field (they break the render) — rephrase instead.
- {{user}}'s own lines are typed by the player; only ever render the AI's output, never {{user}}'s.
- Language: write the AI's words in {{user}}'s roleplay language; the Name and #hex stay constant regardless of language.

## EXAMPLE
[AITHINK|#8fe9ff|Cross-referencing the door logs — three entries were wiped an hour ago.]
[AICHAR|VESPER|#8fe9ff]
[AISAY|#8fe9ff|Good evening. Someone scrubbed the east-wing access records. Shall I restore them?]

## SELF-CHECK (every AI block must pass)
1. Is the speaker actually the AI / System {{user}} is talking to (NOT a normal NPC or the main character)?
2. Are [AITHINK] (if used), [AICHAR], [AISAY] consecutive touching lines, in that order, with nothing between them?
3. Is the SAME #hex on all three tags, and free of | and ] in every text field?
If any fails, fix it BEFORE sending.
